text.title: nodejs-express-fastify Core Rules
text.summary: Eight testable rules covering middleware order, schemas, error flow, and shutdown.

rule.statement: Register middleware in this order: helmet, cors, body parsing, logging, rate-limit, routes, 404 handler, error handler. Error handler MUST be last.
rule.rationale: Out-of-order middleware breaks security headers, leaves error handler unable to capture downstream throws.

rule.statement: Never register errorHandler before routes. An Express error handler (4-arg) is only invoked when a route calls next(err).
rule.rationale: Express's middleware ordering rule — error handler must be the last in the chain.

rule.statement: Every route accepting body/params/query must validate against a schema. Zod for Express; TypeBox for Fastify.
rule.rationale: Without runtime validation, TS types lie at the network boundary.

rule.statement: In Fastify, compile schemas at module scope, not inside the handler registration.
rule.rationale: Fastify caches compiled schemas per definition; per-request compile = 10x slowdown.

rule.statement: All errors flow to the centralized error handler via next(err) (Express) or by throwing (Fastify). Never call res.status().json() with an error shape in a handler.
rule.rationale: Centralized error handling is the only way to keep response shapes consistent (see [[error-handling]]).

rule.statement: In Express 4, wrap async handlers with try/catch and call next(err) in the catch. In Express 5+, unhandled promise rejections propagate automatically.
rule.rationale: Express 4 swallows unhandled promise rejections; explicit wrapping prevents silent 500s.

rule.statement: Wrap any Fastify plugin that decorates the instance with fastify-plugin (fp) when the decorator must be visible outside the plugin scope.
rule.rationale: Fastify encapsulates by default; fastify-plugin exposes decorations upward.

rule.statement: Listen for SIGTERM and SIGINT. On signal: stop accepting new connections, drain in-flight via server.close(), close DB pools, then exit.
rule.rationale: Containers/k8s send SIGTERM; ignoring it drops in-flight requests on rollout.

rule.statement: Middleware ordered correctly, error handler last, schemas at module scope, errors via next(err)/throw, SIGTERM listener wired.
Not Node HTTP, or no long-lived lifecycle (serverless).
